stories.title: Thunder Sweep Lakers — OKC Advances to WCF Perfect 8-0

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
SWEEP. Thunder 115, Lakers 110.

SGA 35 pts. Holmgren tiebreaking dunk with 33 seconds left. Ajay Mitchell 28 pts (10 in Q4).

Oklahoma City is a PERFECT 8-0 in the 2026 playoffs. #ThunderUp

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
LeBron James: 24 pts, 14 reb. Missed a bank shot with 20 seconds left that could've tied it.

His 23rd NBA season ends with a second-round sweep.

The era may be ending. #LakeShow #ThunderUp

facebook.long_form:
Oklahoma City is on another level.

The Thunder completed a four-game sweep of the Los Angeles Lakers Monday night with a 115-110 win that was every bit as dramatic as the final score suggests. Shai Gilgeous-Alexander delivered 35 points to lead OKC, but the play that sealed it was Chet Holmgren's tiebreaking dunk with just 32.8 seconds left on the clock. Then Ajay Mitchell — who poured in 28 points including 10 in the fourth quarter — helped close it out as the Thunder survived a LeBron-led Lakers push.

LeBron James fought hard in what may have been the last game of his career. He scored 24 points, hauled in 14 rebounds, and had the ball in his hands in the final seconds — but a driving bank shot with 20 seconds left rimmed out, and OKC sealed the win at the free-throw line.

Oklahoma City is now a perfect 8-0 in the 2026 playoffs and advances to the Western Conference Finals. They'll await the winner of the Spurs-Timberwolves series, which is tied 2-2 with Game 5 tonight.

This Thunder squad is special. Eight wins, zero losses.

Are you picking OKC to win the title? Drop it in the comments.
facebook.image_caption: OKC sweeps the Lakers 4-0. SGA + Holmgren are making history. 8-0 in the playoffs. The Thunder are BUILT DIFFERENT. #ThunderUp

article.headline: Thunder Sweep Lakers, Advance to WCF With Perfect 8-0 Record

stories.title: Donovan Mitchell 43-Point Masterpiece — Cavs Even Series 2-2

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Donovan Mitchell: 4 pts at halftime → 43 total.

His 39-pt second half TIES the 1987 NBA playoff record by Eric Floyd.

The Cavs went on a 24-0 run spanning halftime. Cleveland wins 112-103. Series tied 2-2. #LetEmKnow

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
1-for-8 in the first half. 12-for-18 in the second.

Mitchell scored 15 of Cleveland's 24 unanswered points during a halftime-spanning run.

SPIDA was a different player after the break. Series even. #CavsNation

facebook.long_form:
You could not have scripted this game if you tried.

Donovan Mitchell scored just 4 points in the first half — going 1-for-8 from the field — and the Cavaliers trailed the Detroit Pistons heading into halftime. Then something clicked.

In the second half, Mitchell was an entirely different player. He poured in 39 points — tying the 1987 NBA playoff record for most points in a half, set by Eric "Sleepy" Floyd of the Golden State Warriors. The Cavaliers went on a 24-0 run that started with the last 12 seconds of the first half and stretched through the first six minutes of the third quarter. Mitchell had 15 of those 24 unanswered points.

By the time the dust settled: Mitchell had 43 total points. The Cavs won 112-103. The series is now tied 2-2.

The Pistons had been in full control just 24 hours ago with a 2-1 series lead. Now Game 5 heads to Detroit on Wednesday with all the momentum flowing Cleveland's way.

This is what playoff basketball looks like.

What's your take — can the Pistons bounce back in Game 5?
facebook.image_caption: Donovan Mitchell: 4 pts at halftime. 39 in the second half. 43 total. 24-0 run. Series tied. SPIDA does NOT quit. #LetEmKnow

article.headline: Mitchell's 39-Point Half Ties 1987 Record as Cavs Even Series 2-2 With 24-0 Run

stories.title: Spurs vs. Timberwolves Game 5 Tonight — Wemby Returns to San Antonio

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Game 5 TONIGHT. Spurs vs. Timberwolves — 8:00 PM ET on NBC.

Series tied 2-2. Wemby back on his home floor in San Antonio.

Edwards coming off 36 pts in Game 4. Don't miss this one. #Spurs #Timberwolves

facebook.long_form:
It's the series nobody expected to be THIS good.

The San Antonio Spurs and Minnesota Timberwolves square off in a pivotal Game 5 TONIGHT at 8:00 PM ET on NBC from the AT&T Center in San Antonio. The series is tied 2-2.

Here's where it stands:
— Spurs won Game 1
— Spurs won Game 2 by 38 (Minnesota's worst playoff loss ever)
— Wembanyama had 39 pts and 15 reb to win Game 3 for San Antonio
— In Game 4, Wemby was ejected in the first half after a Flagrant 2 foul, and Edwards exploded for 36 points with 16 in the fourth quarter to drag Minnesota to victory

Tonight it resets. Wemby back in front of his home fans. Edwards looking to keep rolling.

This one has All-Star matchup written all over it. Don't miss it tonight.

Who wins Game 5?
facebook.image_caption: Game 5 TONIGHT. Spurs vs. Wolves. 8:00 PM ET on NBC. Wemby vs. Edwards. Series tied 2-2. Who ya got? #Spurs #Timberwolves

article.headline: Spurs-Wolves Game 5 Tonight: Wemby Returns to San Antonio With Series on the Line

stories.title: Cavaliers-Pistons Game 5 in Detroit — Momentum Battle Wednesday

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The Pistons had a 2-1 series lead 24 hours ago.

Now it's 2-2, and Cleveland has ALL the momentum after Mitchell's historic night.

Game 5 heads to Detroit on Wednesday. Can Cade and the Pistons respond? #DetroitBasketball #CavsNation

facebook.long_form:
Twenty-four hours. That's all it took to completely flip this series.

After taking a 2-1 lead, Detroit looked positioned to advance. Then Donovan Mitchell had one of the greatest second halves in NBA playoff history, the Cavs went on a 24-0 run, and just like that — it's all tied up.

Game 5 moves to Little Caesars Arena in Detroit on Wednesday. The Pistons will have their home crowd behind them, but the momentum belongs entirely to Cleveland right now.

Cade Cunningham has been excellent throughout this series — 23/7 in Game 1, 25/10 in Game 2 — and the Pistons will need him to answer the call in a must-control game. For Cleveland, Mitchell showed he can take over when it matters most.

This series just got a whole lot more interesting.

Which team survives to face the Knicks?
facebook.image_caption: Pistons had a 2-1 lead. 24 hours later it's 2-2 and Cleveland has ALL the momentum. Game 5 is Wednesday in Detroit. #DetroitBasketball #CavsNation

article.headline: Cavs-Pistons Game 5 Preview: Detroit Must Respond After Mitchell's Historic Explosion

stories.title: LeBron James After Sweep — A 23rd Season Ends, Questions Begin

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
23 seasons. All-time leading scorer.

Last night LeBron James missed the bank shot that could've kept the Lakers alive. His 23rd season is over.

No retirement announcement yet — but this feels like a crossroads. #LakeShow

facebook.long_form:
This is the hardest part of watching LeBron James.

Monday night, with the Lakers down by three and 20 seconds on the clock, LeBron drove the lane and launched a banking shot that could have tied the game. It didn't fall. The Thunder sealed it at the line. And just like that — the 2026 season was over for Los Angeles, and quite possibly, for LeBron himself.

Think about what he did this year alone: 24 points and 14 rebounds in his final playoff game. In his 23rd NBA season. At 41 years old. He's the all-time leading scorer in NBA history. He has four championships, four MVP awards, and has played in the Finals more times than most players appear in All-Star games.

No retirement announcement has been made. But the sweep by Oklahoma City, the age, the contract situation — none of it points toward a 24th season as a certainty.

Whether you're the biggest LeBron fan or you've argued against him your entire life, you have to acknowledge what you just witnessed across 23 seasons of professional basketball. There may never be another like him.

How do you want to remember LeBron James?
facebook.image_caption: 23 seasons. All-time scoring leader. Last night may have been LeBron's final playoff game. The greatest career in NBA history deserves a proper sendoff. #LakeShow #LeBronJames

article.headline: After 23 Seasons, LeBron James Faces Uncertain Future After Lakers Are Swept
